page: Microsoft Azure FX12ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture virtual server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 12 vCPUs mapped from 6 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The hardware configuration is highly memory-dense, providing 252.0 GB of RAM, which yields 21.0 GB of memory per core. The processor is equipped with a large 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, alongside support for Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX).

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core capabilities. The server achieves a top-tier Geekbench single-core score of 2349.0 and a text processing score of 3015.0, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Single-threaded gzip compression reaches 34.88 GB/sec, and bzip3 decompression performs at 14.47 GB/sec, representing strong top-25% performance. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 11866.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 13337.6. Memory bandwidth is measured at 1037.5 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 94.15 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

From a resource density perspective, the FX12ms_v2 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute. However, a key hardware tradeoff is the total absence of local storage, requiring reliance on external premium storage options. Additionally, there are no bundled public IPv4 addresses or GPU accelerators, which limits its utility for direct-to-consumer web hosting or heavy graphics processing.

This server configuration is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and text processing tasks. The inclusion of AMX instructions enables efficient small-scale LLM inference, as demonstrated by a top-tier text generation speed of 339.89 tokens/sec on a 135M parameter model. It is also appropriate for single-threaded compression tasks and memory-cached applications like Redis, which performs at 18.98 million operations per second.
description: The Microsoft Azure FX12ms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 12 vCPUs and 252.0 GB of RAM, powered by a dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 21.0 GB per core and a substantial 260 MB L3 cache. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying instead on remote premium storage. Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2349.0 and strong single-threaded compression speeds. Conversely, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics align with average tiers. The server is suited for memory-intensive databases, text processing, single-threaded compression, and small-scale LLM inference utilizing its built-in Advanced Matrix Extensions.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX12ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 12 vCPUs, 252 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU. It features top-tier single-core performance but lacks local storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX12ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 12 vCPUs, 252 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, suited for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring high-frequency single-core performance and advanced matrix extensions for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 12 vCPUs and 252 GB of RAM providing 21 GB per core
Dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 260 MB L3 cache
Top-tier single-core performance and text processing benchmarks
No local storage, requiring external premium storage solutions
Supports AVX-512 and AMX instructions for accelerated workloads
Best suited for memory-intensive databases and text processing
